sections.paragraph:
- Original: πλήθω

- Transliteration: Pletho

- Phonetic: play'-tho

- Definition:

1. to fill

2. to be fulfilled, to be filled

- Origin: a prolonged form of a primary pleo pleh'-o (which appears only as an alternate in certain tenses and in the reduplicated form pimplemi)

- TDNT entry: 6:128,*

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: A prolonged form of a primary word πλέω pleō (which appears only as an alternate in certain tenses and in the reduplicated form of πίμπλημι pimplē mi to fill (literally or figuratively [imbue influence supply]); specifically to fulfil (time): - accomplish full (... come) furnish.
